revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称=宇賀神社 |画像=[[File:Ugajinja.jpg|280px|宇賀神社(千葉県香取郡多古町)]] |所在地=[[千葉県]][[香取郡]][[多古町]]飯笹688 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 45 | 緯度秒 = 37.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 26 | 経度秒 = 43.0 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神=倉稲魂命 |社格= |創建=応永10年 |本殿= |例祭= |神事= }} '''宇賀神…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=宇賀神社 |画像=[[File:Ugajinja.jpg|280px|宇賀神社(千葉県香取郡多古町)]] |所在地=[[千葉県]][[香取郡]][[多古町]]飯笹688 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 45 | 緯度秒 = 37.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 26 | 経度秒 = 43.0 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神=倉稲魂命 |社格= |創建=応永10年 |本殿= |例祭= |神事= }} '''宇賀神社'''（うがじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[香取郡]][[多古町]]飯笹にある神社である。
==由緒== 「社寺明細帳」には、祭神[[倉稲魂命]]、応永10年（1403年）勧請と記されている。
改修記念碑文には、[[推古天皇]]の御代（593-623年）に田村王の奏上により、飯笹村字稲荷山に遷座し、[[慶長]]14年（1609年）社殿を造営して現在地に奉遷したとある<ref>『多古町ぶらり散歩』 多古町教育委員会 P59</ref>。
ある時代には、村社として近隣9か村の総鎮守となっており、毎年[[神楽]]が奉納されて、その行列が通った旧坂に「神楽坂」の名がついたといわれている。
境内林も広いうえに巨木が多く、大正のころまでは飯笹の鎮守の森として広く知られていたという<ref>『多古町名所百選』 多古町教育委員会 P38</ref>。
==アクセス== *JR[[成田駅]]・[[八日市場駅]]から[[JRバス関東]] 飯笹下車 徒歩15分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:うかしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:多古町の建築物]] [[Category:多古町の歴史]]

[[ja:%E5%AE%87%E8%B3%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E5%8F%A4%E7%94%BA%E9%A3%AF%E7%AC%B9)]] [[Category:Mass editing to refresh the first new Wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] '''Uga Shrine''' (宇賀神社)
== Overview == '''Uga Shrine''' (うがじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Iifusa, {{ill|Tako Town|ja|多古町}}, {{ill|Katori District|ja|香取郡}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan.
== History == According to the "Shaji Meisaicho" (Register of Shrines and Temples), the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and the shrine was established in the 10th year of the Oei era (1403).
An inscription on a monument commemorating a renovation states that during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} (593–623), the shrine was relocated to Inariyama in Iifusa Village following a petition by {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村王}}. In the 14th year of the Keicho era (1609), the shrine buildings were constructed, and the shrine was transferred to its current location.<sup>1</sup>
At one time, the shrine served as the village shrine and was the general guardian deity for nine neighboring villages. It is said that a {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽}} performance was dedicated annually, and the old slope where the procession passed was named "{{ill|Kagurazaka|ja|神楽坂}}."
The shrine grounds are spacious and feature many large trees. Until around the Taisho era, it was widely known as the sacred grove ({{ill|Chinju no Mori|ja|鎮守の森}}) of Iifusa.<sup>2</sup>
== Access == * From {{ill|Narita Station|ja|成田駅}} or {{ill|Yokaichiba Station|ja|八日市場駅}}, take the {{ill|JR Bus Kanto|ja|JRバス関東}} and get off at Iifusa. The shrine is a 15-minute walk from the bus stop.
== References == {{reflist}}
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tako, Chiba]] [[Category:History of Tako, Chiba]]

[[ja:%E5%AE%87%E8%B3%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E5%8F%A4%E7%94%BA%E9%A3%AF%E7%AC%B9)]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Uga Shrine (Tako Town, Iizasa)}}{{citation needed|Uga Shrine (Tako Town, Iizasa)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Mass editing to refresh the first new Wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Uga Shrine (Tako Town, Iizasa)}}{{citation needed|Uga Shrine (Tako Town, Iizasa)}}[[Category:11]] '''Uga Shrine''' (宇賀神社)
== Overview == '''Uga Shrine''' (うがじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Iifusa, {{ill|Tako Town|ja|多古町}}, {{ill|Katori District|ja|香取郡}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan.
== History == According to the "Shaji Meisaicho" (Register of Shrines and Temples), the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and the shrine was established in the 10th year of the Oei era (1403).
An inscription on a monument commemorating a renovation states that during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} (593–623), the shrine was relocated to Inariyama in Iifusa Village following a petition by {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村王}}. In the 14th year of the Keicho era (1609), the shrine buildings were constructed, and the shrine was transferred to its current location.<sup>1</sup>
At one time, the shrine served as the village shrine and was the general guardian deity for nine neighboring villages. It is said that a {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽}} performance was dedicated annually, and the old slope where the procession passed was named "{{ill|Kagurazaka|ja|神楽坂}}."
The shrine grounds are spacious and feature many large trees. Until around the Taisho era, it was widely known as the sacred grove ({{ill|Chinju no Mori|ja|鎮守の森}}) of Iifusa.<sup>2</sup>
== Access == * From {{ill|Narita Station|ja|成田駅}} or {{ill|Yokaichiba Station|ja|八日市場駅}}, take the {{ill|JR Bus Kanto|ja|JRバス関東}} and get off at Iifusa. The shrine is a 15-minute walk from the bus stop.

An inscription on a monument commemorating a renovation states that during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} (593–623), the shrine was relocated to Inariyama in Iifusa Village following a petition by {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村王}}. In the 14th year of the Keicho era (1609), the shrine buildings were constructed, and the shrine was transferred to its current location.<sup>1</sup>
At one time, the shrine served as the village shrine and was the general guardian deity for nine neighboring villages. It is said that a {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽}} performance was dedicated annually, and the old slope where the procession passed was named "{{ill|Kagurazaka|ja|神楽坂}}."
The shrine grounds are spacious and feature many large trees. Until around the Taisho era, it was widely known as the sacred grove ({{ill|Chinju no Mori|ja|鎮守の森}}) of Iifusa.<sup>2</sup>
== Access == * From {{ill|Narita Station|ja|成田駅}} or {{ill|Yokaichiba Station|ja|八日市場駅}}, take the {{ill|JR Bus Kanto|ja|JRバス関東}} and get off at Iifusa. The shrine is a 15-minute walk from the bus stop.

5 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:宇賀神社 (多古町飯笹) up to rev 97884795
revision.text:
{{otheruses|千葉県多古町飯笹にある神社|同名の神社|宇賀神社}} {{神社 |名称=宇賀神社 |画像=[[File:Ugajinja.jpg|280px|宇賀神社(千葉県香取郡多古町)]] |所在地=[[千葉県]][[香取郡]][[多古町]]飯笹688 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 45 | 緯度秒 = 37.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 26 | 経度秒 = 43.0 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神=倉稲魂命 |社格= |創建=応永10年 |本殿= |例祭= |神事= }} '''宇賀神社'''（うがじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[香取郡]][[多古町]]飯笹にある神社である。
==由緒== 「社寺明細帳」には、祭神[[倉稲魂命]]、応永10年（1403年）勧請と記されている。
改修記念碑文には、[[推古天皇]]の御代（593-623年）に田村王の奏上により、飯笹村字稲荷山に遷座し、[[慶長]]14年（1609年）社殿を造営して現在地に奉遷したとある<ref>『多古町ぶらり散歩』 多古町教育委員会 P59</ref>。
ある時代には、村社として近隣9か村の総鎮守となっており、毎年[[神楽]]が奉納されて、その行列が通った旧坂に「神楽坂」の名がついたといわれている。
境内林も広いうえに巨木が多く、大正のころまでは飯笹の鎮守の森として広く知られていたという<ref>『多古町名所百選』 多古町教育委員会 P38</ref>。
==アクセス== *JR[[成田駅]]・[[八日市場駅]]から[[JRバス関東]] 飯笹下車 徒歩15分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:うかしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:多古町の建築物]] [[Category:多古町の歴史]]

'''Uga Shrine''' (宇賀神社)
== Overview == '''Uga Shrine''' (うがじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Iifusa, [[:en:Tako, Chiba|Tako Town]], [[:en:Katori District|Katori District]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == According to the "Shaji Meisaicho" (Register of Shrines and Temples), the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, and the shrine was established in the 10th year of the Oei era (1403).
An inscription on a monument commemorating a renovation states that during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (593–623), the shrine was relocated to Inariyama in Iifusa Village following a petition by {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the 14th year of the Keicho era (1609), the shrine buildings were constructed, and the shrine was transferred to its current location.<sup>1</sup>
At one time, the shrine served as the village shrine and was the general guardian deity for nine neighboring villages. It is said that a [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] performance was dedicated annually, and the old slope where the procession passed was named "[[:en:Kagurazaka|Kagurazaka]]."
The shrine grounds are spacious and feature many large trees. Until around the Taisho era, it was widely known as the sacred grove ([[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]]) of Iifusa.<sup>2</sup>
== Access == * From [[:en:Narita Station|Narita Station]] or [[:en:Yōkaichiba Station|Yokaichiba Station]], take the {{ill|JR Bus Kanto|ja|JRバス関東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Bus Kanto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ジェイアールバス関東}} and get off at Iifusa. The shrine is a 15-minute walk from the bus stop.

'''Uga Shrine''' (宇賀神社)
== Overview == '''Uga Shrine''' (うがじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Iifusa, [[:en:Tako, Chiba|Tako Town]], [[:en:Katori District|Katori District]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == According to the "Shaji Meisaicho" (Register of Shrines and Temples), the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, and the shrine was established in the 10th year of the Oei era (1403).
An inscription on a monument commemorating a renovation states that during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (593–623), the shrine was relocated to Inariyama in Iifusa Village following a petition by {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the 14th year of the Keicho era (1609), the shrine buildings were constructed, and the shrine was transferred to its current location.<sup>1</sup>
At one time, the shrine served as the village shrine and was the general guardian deity for nine neighboring villages. It is said that a [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] performance was dedicated annually, and the old slope where the procession passed was named "[[:en:Kagurazaka|Kagurazaka]]."
The shrine grounds are spacious and feature many large trees. Until around the Taisho era, it was widely known as the sacred grove ([[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]]) of Iifusa.<sup>2</sup>
== Access == * From [[:en:Narita Station|Narita Station]] or [[:en:Yōkaichiba Station|Yokaichiba Station]], take the {{ill|JR Bus Kanto|ja|JRバス関東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Bus Kanto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ジェイアールバス関東}} and get off at Iifusa. The shrine is a 15-minute walk from the bus stop.
== References == {{reflist}}
]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Uga Shrine''' (宇賀神社)
== Overview == '''Uga Shrine''' (うがじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Iifusa, [[:en:Tako, Chiba|Tako Town]], [[:en:Katori District|Katori District]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == According to the "Shaji Meisaicho" (Register of Shrines and Temples), the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, and the shrine was established in the 10th year of the Oei era (1403).
An inscription on a monument commemorating a renovation states that during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (593–623), the shrine was relocated to Inariyama in Iifusa Village following a petition by {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the 14th year of the Keicho era (1609), the shrine buildings were constructed, and the shrine was transferred to its current location.<sup>1</sup>
At one time, the shrine served as the village shrine and was the general guardian deity for nine neighboring villages. It is said that a [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] performance was dedicated annually, and the old slope where the procession passed was named "[[:en:Kagurazaka|Kagurazaka]]."
The shrine grounds are spacious and feature many large trees. Until around the Taisho era, it was widely known as the sacred grove ([[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]]) of Iifusa.<sup>2</sup>
== Access == * From [[:en:Narita Station|Narita Station]] or [[:en:Yōkaichiba Station|Yokaichiba Station]], take the {{ill|JR Bus Kanto|ja|JRバス関東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Bus Kanto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ジェイアールバス関東}} and get off at Iifusa. The shrine is a 15-minute walk from the bus stop.
== References == {{reflist}}

== History == According to the "Shaji Meisaicho" (Register of Shrines and Temples), the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, and the shrine was established in the 10th year of the Oei era (1403).
An inscription on a monument commemorating a renovation states that during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (593–623), the shrine was relocated to Inariyama in Iifusa Village following a petition by {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the 14th year of the Keicho era (1609), the shrine buildings were constructed, and the shrine was transferred to its current location.<sup>1</sup>
At one time, the shrine served as the village shrine and was the general guardian deity for nine neighboring villages. It is said that a [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] performance was dedicated annually, and the old slope where the procession passed was named "[[:en:Kagurazaka|Kagurazaka]]."
The shrine grounds are spacious and feature many large trees. Until around the Taisho era, it was widely known as the sacred grove ([[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]]) of Iifusa.<sup>2</sup>
== Access == * From [[:en:Narita Station|Narita Station]] or [[:en:Yōkaichiba Station|Yokaichiba Station]], take the {{ill|JR Bus Kanto|ja|JRバス関東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Bus Kanto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ジェイアールバス関東|1=JR Bus Kanto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10851155}} and get off at Iifusa. The shrine is a 15-minute walk from the bus stop.

== History == According to the "Shaji Meisaicho" (Register of Shrines and Temples), the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, and the shrine was established in the 10th year of the Oei era (1403).
An inscription on a monument commemorating a renovation states that during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (593–623), the shrine was relocated to Inariyama in Iifusa Village following a petition by {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the 14th year of the Keicho era (1609), the shrine buildings were constructed, and the shrine was transferred to its current location.<sup>1</sup>
At one time, the shrine served as the village shrine and was the general guardian deity for nine neighboring villages. It is said that a [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] performance was dedicated annually, and the old slope where the procession passed was named "[[:en:Kagurazaka|Kagurazaka]]."
The shrine grounds are spacious and feature many large trees. Until around the Taisho era, it was widely known as the sacred grove ([[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]]) of Iifusa.<sup>2</sup>
== Access == * From [[:en:Narita Station|Narita Station]] or [[:en:Yōkaichiba Station|Yokaichiba Station]], take the {{ill|JR Bus Kanto|ja|JRバス関東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Bus Kanto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ジェイアールバス関東|1=JR Bus Kanto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10851155}} and get off at Iifusa. The shrine is a 15-minute walk from the bus stop.

An inscription on a monument commemorating a renovation states that during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (593–623), the shrine was relocated to Inariyama in Iifusa Village following a petition by {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the 14th year of the Keicho era (1609), the shrine buildings were constructed, and the shrine was transferred to its current location.<sup>1</sup>
At one time, the shrine served as the village shrine and was the general guardian deity for nine neighboring villages. It is said that a [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] performance was dedicated annually, and the old slope where the procession passed was named "[[:en:Kagurazaka|Kagurazaka]]."
The shrine grounds are spacious and feature many large trees. Until around the Taisho era, it was widely known as the sacred grove ([[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]]) of Iifusa.<sup>2</sup>
== Access == * From [[:en:Narita Station|Narita Station]] or [[:en:Yōkaichiba Station|Yokaichiba Station]], take the {{ill|JR Bus Kanto|ja|JRバス関東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Bus Kanto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ジェイアールバス関東|1=JR Bus Kanto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10851155}} and get off at Iifusa. The shrine is a 15-minute walk from the bus stop.

An inscription on a monument commemorating a renovation states that during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (593–623), the shrine was relocated to Inariyama in Iifusa Village following a petition by {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the 14th year of the Keicho era (1609), the shrine buildings were constructed, and the shrine was transferred to its current location.<sup>1</sup>
At one time, the shrine served as the village shrine and was the general guardian deity for nine neighboring villages. It is said that a [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] performance was dedicated annually, and the old slope where the procession passed was named "[[:en:Kagurazaka|Kagurazaka]]."
The shrine grounds are spacious and feature many large trees. Until around the Taisho era, it was widely known as the sacred grove ([[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]]) of Iifusa.<sup>2</sup>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇賀神社 (多古町飯笹)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇賀神社 (多古町飯笹)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇賀神社 (多古町飯笹)]] [[ja:宇賀神社 (多古町飯笹)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}{{Infobox religious building|image=Ugajinja.jpg}}
'''Uga Shrine''' (うがじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Iifusa, {{ill|Tako, Chiba|en|Tako, Chiba|WD=Q1202807|lt=Tako Town}}, {{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan.
== History == According to the "Shaji Meisaicho" (Register of Shrines and Temples), the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, and the shrine was established in the 10th year of the Oei era (1403).
An inscription on a monument commemorating a renovation states that during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} (593–623), the shrine was relocated to Inariyama in Iifusa Village following a petition by {{ill|Prince Tamura|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tamura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the 14th year of the Keicho era (1609), the shrine buildings were constructed, and the shrine was transferred to its current location.<sup>1</sup>
At one time, the shrine served as the village shrine and was the general guardian deity for nine neighboring villages. It is said that a {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kagura}} performance was dedicated annually, and the old slope where the procession passed was named "{{ill|Kagurazaka|en|Kagurazaka|WD=Q3275957|lt=Kagurazaka}}."
The shrine grounds are spacious and feature many large trees. Until around the Taisho era, it was widely known as the sacred grove ({{ill|Chinju no Mori|en|Chinju no Mori|WD=Q11651158|lt=Chinju no Mori}}) of Iifusa.<sup>2</sup>
== Access == * From {{ill|Narita Station|en|Narita Station|WD=Q845243|lt=Narita Station}} or {{ill|Yōkaichiba Station|en|Yōkaichiba Station|WD=Q5363766|lt=Yokaichiba Station}}, take the {{ill|JR Bus Kanto|ja|JRバス関東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Bus Kanto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ジェイアールバス関東|1=JR Bus Kanto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10851155}} and get off at Iifusa. The shrine is a 15-minute walk from the bus stop.
== References == {{reflist}}
{{translated page|ja|宇賀神社 (多古町飯笹)|version=97884795|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[en:Uga Shrine (Tako Town, Iizasa)]]

'''Uga Shrine''' (うがじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Iifusa, {{ill|Tako, Chiba|en|Tako, Chiba|WD=Q1202807|lt=Tako Town}}, {{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan.

It is said that a {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kagura}} performance was dedicated annually, and the old slope where the procession passed was named "{{ill|Kagurazaka|en|Kagurazaka|WD=Q3275957|lt=Kagurazaka}}."
